pattern: Running the moves in reverse -- halving on even values and decrementing on odd ones -- collapses a huge forward search into a handful of arithmetic steps.
hints: Instead of building up from 1, imagine tearing the target back down to 1 instead.
While a double is still available, undo a double by halving whenever the number is even, and undo an increment by subtracting one whenever it's odd; you can't halve an odd number.

Once the double budget hits zero, the rest of the trip back to 1 is nothing but one-at-a-time subtraction, so it collapses into a single arithmetic step: whatever the number is at that point, minus one.

edge_inputs.description: no doubles allowed at all, pure increments
a normal mix of increments and doubles
already at the target, doubles left unused
already at the target with the maximum double budget
largest legal target with no doubles at all
largest legal target with the maximum double budget
odd target forces a decrement before a double can be used
more doubles available than are ever useful

stress.description: random target and double budget across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    target = rng.randint(1, size)
    max_doubles = rng.randint(0, 100)
    return [target, max_doubles]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minMoves(self, target, maxDoubles):
        # Independent bit-counting derivation of the same answer: the
        # highest set bit position h is how many halvings a full walk down
        # to 1 needs. If maxDoubles covers all of them, moves are exactly
        # h halvings plus one decrement per remaining 1-bit. Otherwise the
        # double budget only reaches the lowest maxDoubles bits, and the
        # rest of the distance is pure decrementing.
        if target == 1:
            return 0
        h = target.bit_length() - 1
        if maxDoubles >= h:
            return h + bin(target).count('1') - 1
        mask = (1 << maxDoubles) - 1
        low_ones = bin(target & mask).count('1')
        remaining = target >> maxDoubles
        return maxDoubles + low_ones + remaining - 1
